# AUTOPLAN REVIEW — Google Docs Resume Template

> **STATUS: ✅ APPROVED (2026-06-27).** Final gate → user chose A (approve all per recommendations).
> Scope = B (hybrid). 27 structural fixes accepted. Taste calls DT1 (directory-first layout) and
> DT2 (distinctive document-forward visual identity) both ACCEPTED. 29-task backlog accepted.
> Next: `/ship` when ready, or start with P1 tasks E1-E6 + the link-acquisition plan.


Plan reviewed: `PRD.md` (v0.1) + `demo.html`
Pipeline: CEO → Design → Eng (DX skipped — consumer product, not a dev tool)
Decision principles: completeness, boil-lakes, pragmatic, DRY, explicit-over-clever, bias-to-action
Restore point: `~/.gstack/projects/google-docs-resume-template/main-autoplan-restore-20260626-232238.md`

# PHASE 1 — CEO REVIEW (Strategy & Scope)

Mode: **SELECTIVE EXPANSION** (hold the user's scope as baseline, surface what's missing).

## 0A. Premise Challenge

The PRD rests on five load-bearing premises. Three are false-as-applied, two are sound.

| # | Premise (from PRD) | Verdict | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | User intent is "task" not "inspiration" — searchers want a template now | **SOUND** | Head term shows no AI Overview, ~86% organic clicks. Transactional intent is real. |
| P2 | Direct Google Docs `/copy` is the differentiator | **FALSE — table stakes** | gdoc.io, thegoodocs, resume.io all do `/copy`. Google's native template gallery does it inside the product with zero clicks. It's the entry ticket, not a moat. |
| P3 | "Vol 32k / KD 12 / low-DR sites in SERP" ⇒ a zero-authority new domain can win | **FALSE — misread** | Live SERP: every link-light winner sits on DR 95-99 (Reddit #1, Google Docs #3, YouTube #6/#10, Pinterest #12). The one low-DR *domain* (resumgo, DR 18) ranks on **417 page-level referring domains**. There is no exemplar in the top 20 that looks like a brand-new site with ~0 links. KD is a backlink-count proxy; it's blind to this UGC/brand fortress. |
| P4 | 24 templates + 30 programmatic role pages = authority | **FALSE — thin content** | Role-page volumes are 30-200/mo each; the whole programmatic layer is ~100-300 incremental visits/mo and carries Google "scaled content abuse" / Helpful-Content risk. |
| P5 | Commercialization path is clear | **WEAK** | Categories exist, but every monetization mode needs traffic the plan won't have for 12+ months, and the $9-19 pack monetizes an audience that self-selected for "free." |

**The binding constraint the PRD never names: backlinks / domain authority.** Ranking in this SERP is gated on links, not content volume. The plan has a link strategy of "post on Reddit once."

## 0B. Existing Code Leverage

Greenfield repo (empty before this session). Nothing to reuse internally. Externally:
- `demo.html` is a solid static prototype (gallery, filters, search, preview modal, FAQ, ItemList schema). It is reusable as the head collection page — its UI is fine.
- Astro/Next static export + JSON template data (PRD §12.3) is the right, boring stack. No reason to rebuild.

## 0C. Dream State Mapping

```
CURRENT STATE            THIS PLAN (as written)          12-MONTH IDEAL
empty repo,        --->  static directory, 24 tpl,  -->  a job-search asset with a
zero authority,          30 thin role pages,             defensible wedge (a tool or
no audience              /copy as "moat",                a trusted ATS-tested template
                         ads/affiliate later             brand) + a link engine that
                                                         compounds + distribution on
                                                         Reddit/Pinterest/YT/Docs
```
As written, the plan moves toward "low-margin SEO inventory behind aged incumbents," not toward the ideal. The delta is: **no link engine, no durable differentiator, no distribution-first motion.**

## 0C-bis. Implementation Alternatives (MANDATORY)

```
APPROACH A: Static SEO directory as written (the PRD)
  Summary: 24 templates + 6 category + 30 role pages, /copy CTA, ads/affiliate later.
  Effort:  human ~2-3 weeks / CC ~1-2 days for the site shell + pages
  Risk:    HIGH (ranking risk: no links; trust risk: fake ATS scores; HCU risk: thin pages)
  Pros:    cheap, fast to build, matches a programmatic-SEO playbook
  Cons:    bets on the one asset class that loses in this SERP; no moat; fantasy metrics
  Reuses:  demo.html

APPROACH B: Directory + the non-negotiable fixes (HYBRID)  [RECOMMENDED]
  Summary: keep the SEO directory, but (1) add a real link-acquisition plan as a P0 gate,
           (2) cut 30 role pages to ~5 verified (>=500 traffic-potential), (3) replace fake
           ATS scores with a transparent ATS-safety checklist + real screenshots,
           (4) reset 30/90-day targets to indexation + a few KD 2-3 long-tails.
  Effort:  human ~2-3 weeks / CC ~1-2 days + ongoing link work
  Risk:    MED (removes the fatal flaws; ranking still gated on link execution)
  Pros:    respects the user's SEO strategy; kills the credibility + HCU + fantasy-metric landmines
  Cons:    still competes on incumbents' turf; success still depends on links, which are slow
  Reuses:  demo.html, the whole PRD structure

APPROACH C: Distribution + tool wedge (both models' top pick)
  Summary: 8-10 excellent templates seeded AS the Reddit post / Pinterest board / YouTube
           video / public Google Doc (mirrors the proven #1 on this exact SERP); ONE free
           interactive tool (ATS checker or JD->bullet generator) as the link magnet and
           differentiator; one head collection page on-domain; directory = conversion endpoint.
  Effort:  human ~3-5 weeks / CC ~3-5 days (the tool is real product work)
  Risk:    MED-HIGH (more build, but the only path with a durable moat + a link engine)
  Pros:    differentiated, link-bait, AI-Overview-resistant, first traffic comes faster (parasite SEO)
  Cons:    bigger scope — you're now building an app, not just a content site
  Reuses:  demo.html as the endpoint
```

**RECOMMENDATION: B (hybrid).** It respects the user's stated strategy (a Google Docs resume template SEO site) while removing the three flaws both models independently flagged as fatal: no links, thin programmatic pages, and fake ATS scores. C is the stronger long-term product but changes the nature of the project into app-building; offer it, don't impose it. A as-written ships known landmines.

## 0D. Mode-Specific Analysis (SELECTIVE EXPANSION)

Complexity check: the plan is not over-engineered (static site, no backend) — good. The problem is mis-targeted scope, not too much scope.

Cherry-pick candidates (each is a should-do the PRD missed; presented as findings, decided at the gate):
1. **Link-acquisition plan** (the binding constraint) — digital PR, HARO, university/bootcamp career-center outreach, template embeds with attribution, Product Hunt/SideProject launch. Effort: M, ongoing.
2. **One interactive tool** (ATS checker on a pasted resume, or JD→bullet generator) — the only real differentiator + link magnet. Effort: M (CC), L (human).
3. **Distribution-first seeding** — be the Reddit post / Pinterest board / YouTube video / public Google Doc. Effort: S, high leverage.
4. **Real ATS-safety methodology** (replace the fake "95" score) — transparent checklist + actual PDF/DOCX parse screenshots. Effort: S.

## 0E. Temporal Interrogation

```
HOUR 1 (foundations):  Which ONE head page is the on-domain SEO bet? (Answer: the /copy
                        collection page for "google docs resume template" / "resume template
                        google docs" — KD 8, vol 17k is the easier, bigger sibling.)
HOUR 2-3 (templates):   Who makes 8-24 ORIGINAL templates that beat Google's native gallery?
                        This is the real cost — original design + ATS-tested + PDF/DOCX checks.
HOUR 4-5 (links):       What is the first link source? (Decide NOW, not "stage 3".)
HOUR 6+ (measure):      What does success at day 90 actually look like? (Reset from "top 20 +
                        2,000 clicks" to "indexed + a few KD 2-3 long-tails in top 20".)
```

## 0F. Mode Confirmation

SELECTIVE EXPANSION, recommended approach **B**. Pending the premise gate (the user decides A/B/C).

## 0.5 — DUAL VOICES (Independent Second Opinions)

Two independent reviewers, neither seeing the other, both seeing only the plan.

**CODEX SAYS (CEO — strategy challenge):** "Do not greenlight as written." `/copy` is a feature not a moat; keyword metrics are assumed not validated; "low DR ⇒ winnable" is a bad inference for a zero-link domain; competes head-on with incumbents; AI Overviews + Google's native gallery underweighted; fake ATS scores are a trust problem; monetization is backwards; 30/90-day targets are a wish. Reframe to tool-first workflow (paste JD → tailored Google Doc), 5-8 real templates for ONE ICP, distribution via communities, scale programmatic SEO only after evidence. Downgrade P0 → P1 validation sprint.

**CLAUDE SUBAGENT (CEO — strategic independence):** Pulled live Ahrefs data. Top 20 is a DR 95-99 UGC/brand wall; #1 is a Reddit side-project post (56k traffic, 4 links). No zero-authority exemplar exists. `/copy` = table stakes. 30 role pages = ~100-300 visits total + HCU risk. Resource allocation inverted (tool deferred to Phase 3 = the only moat built last). Distribution (the proven #1 strategy) relegated to a footnote. Verdict: don't ship as written; reframe to distribution + tool wedge, gate everything on links, target the head term not 30 thin pages. Realistic 12-mo on-domain organic: ~800-2,500 visits/mo, not the PRD's 2,000/mo by day 90.

```
CEO DUAL VOICES — CONSENSUS TABLE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Dimension                            Claude  Codex  Consensus
  ──────────────────────────────────── ─────── ─────── ─────────
  1. Premises valid?                    NO      NO     CONFIRMED (both: P2/P3/P4 false)
  2. Right problem to solve?            reframe reframe CONFIRMED (both: tool+distribution)
  3. Scope calibration correct?        NO      NO     CONFIRMED (both: cut role pages)
  4. Alternatives explored enough?     NO      NO     CONFIRMED (distribution/tool/aged-domain dismissed)
  5. Competitive/market risks covered? NO      NO     CONFIRMED (Google gallery, AIO, incumbent links)
  6. 6-month trajectory sound?         NO      NO     CONFIRMED (metrics unrealistic)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  6/6 CONFIRMED. Unusually strong cross-model consensus: the plan as written
  bets on the one asset class (commodity static directory) that loses in this SERP.
  This rises to a USER CHALLENGE — surfaced at the gate, never auto-decided.
```

## Sections 1-11 (strategy pass)

This is a strategy/PRD review. Strategy-relevant sections get full depth; code-implementation
sections (errors, data shadow paths, perf) are examined here and deferred to Phase 3 (Eng).

- **§1 Architecture (= information architecture):** Hub → category → detail → role → guide is conventional and fine. **Finding:** near-duplicate pages (`simple`/`modern`/`professional`/`free`/`ATS` all "...resume template google docs") will cannibalize and read as thin programmatic SEO. Fix: one canonical hub + 5-6 genuinely distinct pages; add role pages only post-links with verified demand. (Carried to Eng §1.)
- **§2 Error & Rescue Map:** N/A at strategy level — static site. One real runtime failure mode: a Google Docs `/copy` link breaks/gets revoked → user hits a dead doc. Must be monitored (link-checker). Deferred to Eng.
- **§3 Security & Threat Model:** Low surface (static, no auth, no PII unless email capture). Email capture (when added) needs spam/validation handling. Trademark/IP handled in PRD §13 (good). **Finding:** fake "ATS score 95" is a *credibility* risk (borderline deceptive) more than a security one. Fix: real methodology or remove.
- **§4 Data Flow & Interaction Edge Cases:** The one real flow is filter/search → card → preview → copy. Edge cases (zero search results, broken copy link, mobile preview) reviewed in Design (Phase 2).
- **§5 Code Quality:** N/A pre-implementation. demo.html is clean. Deferred to Eng.
- **§6 Test Review:** No app logic yet; the "test" that matters is SEO/analytics validation. Deferred to Eng (CTR events, link-checker, schema validation).
- **§7 Performance:** PRD §12.2 targets (LCP <2.0s, CLS <0.05, lazy-load, WebP) are correct for a static site. No issues.
- **§8 Observability:** **Finding:** the most important metric (Copy-in-Google-Docs CTR) and GSC indexation must be wired day 1. PRD §10.2 lists good events. Add: broken-`/copy`-link alerting, GSC coverage monitoring.
- **§9 Deployment:** Static deploy to Vercel/Cloudflare/Netlify is low-risk. Add: sitemap auto-submit to GSC, canonical correctness check, 404/robots/OG (PRD §15 covers this).
- **§10 Long-Term Trajectory:** Reversibility 5/5 (static site, easy to pivot). **Finding:** the trajectory as written plateaus behind incumbents without a link engine. The tool wedge (Approach C) is what creates platform potential.
- **§11 Design & UX:** UI scope detected → full review in Phase 2. (demo.html prototype.)

## Required Outputs

### NOT in scope (deferred)
- 30 programmatic role pages → defer until links exist + verified ≥500 traffic potential (both models: thin-content/HCU risk).
- Login, favorites, online editor, AI generator, payment (PRD §6.4 already defers these — correct).
- $9-19 premium pack → defer/drop (monetizes a "free" audience; near-zero conversion).

### What already exists
- `demo.html` — reusable as the head collection page (UI is fine).
- External: Google Docs `/copy` mechanism (free), Astro/Next static stack (boring, correct).

### Dream state delta
As written, plan ends at "SEO inventory behind aged DR 47-54 incumbents." Gap to ideal: a link engine + one durable differentiator (tool or trusted ATS-tested brand) + distribution-first seeding.

### Failure Modes Registry
```
CODEPATH/ASSET          | FAILURE MODE              | RESCUED? | TEST? | USER SEES        | LOGGED?
------------------------|---------------------------|----------|-------|------------------|--------
Google Docs /copy link  | revoked/edited/deleted    | N ← GAP  | N     | dead document    | N ← GAP
Head term ranking       | stuck pos 30-55 (no links)| N ← GAP  | N     | no traffic       | GSC only
Programmatic role pages | HCU/scaled-content flag   | N ← GAP  | N     | deindexed        | GSC only
Fake ATS score "95"     | user distrust / bounce    | N ← GAP  | N     | invented number  | N ← GAP
```
4 CRITICAL GAPS — all flow from the strategy, not from code. Addressed by Approach B/C choices.

**Verdict:** Do NOT ship `PRD.md` as written at P0. Both models converge: the directory + `/copy` is a commodity; ranking is gated on links the plan doesn't build; fake ATS scores and 30 thin role pages are landmines; metrics are fantasy. Recommended path: **B (directory + non-negotiable fixes)**, with **C (distribution + tool wedge)** offered as the stronger product bet. Awaiting premise-gate decision.

## Premise Gate Outcome — ACCEPTED SCOPE (B: hybrid)

The user resolved the User Challenge by choosing **B**. The plan scope for all remaining
phases (Design, Eng) is now: the Google Docs resume template SEO directory **plus** these
four now-in-scope must-fixes:

1. **Link-acquisition plan (P0)** — the binding ranking constraint. (CEO T1)
2. **Cut 30 programmatic role pages → ~5 verified (≥500 traffic potential)** — kill thin-content/HCU risk. (CEO T3)
3. **Replace fake ATS score (95) with a transparent ATS-safety checklist + real PDF/DOCX parse screenshots.** (CEO T2)
4. **Reset 30/90-day metrics** to indexation + a few KD 2-3 long-tails in top 20. (CEO T6)

Distribution-first seeding (T4) and the interactive tool (T5) remain DEFERRED candidates
(offered as the stronger Approach C, not adopted in B).

# PHASE 2 — DESIGN REVIEW (UI scope: demo.html)

Two independent design voices, neither seeing the other. Codex got the CEO context; the
Claude design subagent got none (true independence). They converge hard.

**CODEX SAYS (design — UX challenge):** "Keep the SEO directory plan, but rebuild the
prototype around evidence and speed." Non-negotiables: remove fake ATS scores; replace
skeletons with real screenshots; replace placeholder copy links with verified links + degraded
states; add empty/error/loading states; make the first viewport a usable template finder, not a
generic landing page. Plus: define the real template data schema now; give every template a
crawlable detail page; fix modal focus management; add `:focus-visible`; treat mobile as design
not reflow.

**CLAUDE SUBAGENT (design — independent):** Overall 3/10. "A good-looking SaaS landing page
wearing a template directory's clothes." Same critical set: dead copy links, identical fake
previews, missing states, no modal focus trap, `outline:none` with no focus-visible, mobile nav
vanishes, internal strategy notes leaking into user copy. Flags one extra structural SEO bug both
the PRD and product depend on: **the grid is client-rendered from a JS array with no static/SSR
fallback and no `<noscript>` — for an SEO product whose premise is crawlable static pages
(PRD §12.1), the core content and core SEO content render as an empty div if JS doesn't run.**

```
DESIGN LITMUS SCORECARD (consensus)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Dimension                  Claude  Codex   Consensus
  ────────────────────────── ─────── ─────── ─────────
  1. Information hierarchy     4/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (templates below fold; hero wastes top pixels)
  2. Interaction states        2/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (empty/error/loading/degraded all missing)
  3. Trust & credibility       3/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (fake score, fake previews, dead links)
  4. User journey / arc        3/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (breaks at grid + at copy click)
  5. Accessibility             3/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (no focus-visible, no modal trap, aria gaps)
  6. Responsive / mobile       4/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (reflow not design; nav vanishes)
  7. AI-slop / distinctiveness 3/10   agrees  CONFIRMED (generic SaaS tropes; fake stat badge)
  ────────────────────────── ─────── ─────── ─────────
  OVERALL                      3/10           7/7 CONFIRMED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Note: demo.html is a PROTOTYPE. A 3/10 prototype is fine as a prototype — these are the
  things to fix when it becomes the real Astro/Next site, not condemnation of the mock.
```

## Auto-decided STRUCTURAL fixes (now in plan scope — both voices, objectively wrong)

| # | Fix | Principle |
|---|-----|-----------|
| D-S1 | Real per-template screenshots replace identical skeletons (card + modal). If a screenshot isn't ready, don't list the template. | completeness |
| D-S2 | Real verified `/copy` links + per-link health state (available/checking/unavailable); broken link degrades to "being updated / try Word", never a dead Google page. | completeness |
| D-S3 | Designed empty/zero-results state with reset + suggestions; loading state for thumbnails. | completeness |
| D-S4 | Static/SSR render of the grid (Astro/Next static per PRD §12.1) + `<noscript>`; hydrate filtering on top. **Also an SEO requirement.** | explicit-over-clever |
| D-S5 | Modal a11y: move focus in, trap Tab, background `inert`, restore focus on close (Escape/click-out already work). | completeness |
| D-S6 | Remove `outline:none` on search; add `:focus-visible` ring on all interactive elements. | completeness |
| D-S7 | Filter chips → `aria-pressed` toggle group; move `aria-live` to a dedicated hidden region announcing a count. | completeness |
| D-S8 | Mobile nav replacement (hamburger/menu) so How-it-works/ATS/FAQ stay reachable. | completeness |
| D-S9 | Delete internal strategy/TODO text from user-facing copy ("In production…", "People Also Ask…", monetization taxonomy panel, "premium pack" in FAQ). | explicit-over-clever |
| D-S10 | Define the real template data schema now (slug, category, roles, experienceLevel, pageCount, thumbnailUrl, previewUrl, copyUrl, docId, lastVerifiedAt, linkStatus, atsChecklist, parseEvidence, caveats). | DRY (carried to Eng §1) |
| D-S11 | Generate JSON-LD from the same source as rendered cards (prototype lists 4 items but renders 9 — keep in sync). | DRY |
| D-S12 | `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` guard for hover transforms + smooth scroll. | completeness |

(D-S1, D-S2, D-S3 are CRITICAL — they are the difference between "looks legit but I can't trust or use it" and "found one, copied it in 10 seconds.")

## TASTE DECISIONS → surfaced at final gate

- **DT1 — Directory-first vs hero-first layout.** Both voices strongly recommend lifting the
  template grid into the first viewport and shrinking the hero (task keyword, not inspiration).
  Defensible to keep a compact hero for SEO context. Recommend: directory-first with a one-band hero.
- **DT2 — Visual language.** Both call the current look "generic SaaS slop" (gradient wash, glass
  header, hover-lift pills, floating stat badge). Recommend: differentiate around real artifacts
  (document screenshots, freshness/"updated 2026" signals, document-library feel). Pure taste —
  the current look is competent, just not distinctive.

## Design Completion Summary
```
+====================================================================+
|            DESIGN REVIEW — COMPLETION SUMMARY                       |
+====================================================================+
| Overall litmus score  | 3/10 (prototype)                           |
| Dimensions confirmed  | 7/7 (both voices agree)                     |
| Structural fixes       | 12 auto-included (3 CRITICAL)              |
| Taste decisions        | 2 → final gate (layout, visual language)   |
| Biggest leverage       | real screenshots + working copy path        |
| Cross-cut to Eng       | data schema (D-S10), static render (D-S4)   |
+====================================================================+
```
**Verdict:** Prototype is a solid skeleton but cannot do its job as-is: user can neither trust
(fake previews/scores) nor act (dead links, no error state). 12 structural fixes auto-included;
2 taste calls (layout, visual identity) go to the final gate.

---

# PHASE 3 — ENG REVIEW (Architecture & Tests)

Two independent eng voices. They converged so hard that BOTH independently named the same
killer insight: **the chosen success metric ("Copy-in-Google-Docs CTR") is structurally blind
to the product's worst failure** — analytics fires on the click, before the destination
resolves, so when a `/copy` link is dead, CTR stays "healthy" while every click bounces to a
dead Google page. You can be 100% broken and the dashboard reads "8% CTR, on target."

**CODEX SAYS (eng — architecture challenge):** "Not ready for implementation as written." The
missing architecture isn't "which framework" — it's the content supply chain and health system
around Docs: typed source-of-truth data → generated route manifest → real build-time HTML →
CI (schema/screenshot/link-health/sitemap/analytics) → nightly external checks. 13-row test
plan + "what breaks at 2am" register.

**CLAUDE SUBAGENT (eng — independent):** "Not ready as written." Same core. Adds: a `/go/{slug}`
**redirect indirection layer** so a dead Doc is a one-line redirect edit (not a full rebuild) and
enables server-side click measurement; **single Google account = catalog-wide single point of
failure**; the prototype's client-rendered grid risks being seeded into production and losing the
entire internal-linking layer programmatic SEO depends on.

## Architecture — real component / data-flow boundary (the "static site" hides 3 subsystems)

```
BUILD TIME (deterministic, offline)              RUN TIME (the actual product)
┌───────────────────────────────┐               ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ templates.json  ◄ source of    │  user click   │ Google Drive (EXTERNAL,      │
│   truth #1 (slug, doc_id,      │── /copy ─────►│ MUTABLE) ◄ source of truth   │
│   copy_url, preview, seo, faq) │   (via /go/    │ #2: Doc content + ACL +      │
│ + /public/images/*.webp        │    {slug})     │ /copy endpoint + login wall  │
└───────────────┬───────────────┘               └──────────────────────────────┘
   SSG (getStaticPaths /                              ▲ drifts independently of build
   generateStaticParams)                              │ NOTHING in the static site
        ▼                                             │ watches this gap
   static HTML routes + sitemap + JSON-LD   ─────────┘
        │
   ── 3 HIDDEN SUBSYSTEMS the PRD hand-waves ──
   (1) screenshot pipeline: Doc -> Drive export -> PDF -> raster -> sharp -> WebP/srcset (+revision hash)
   (2) link-health monitor: scheduled Drive-API/Playwright probe OFF the deploy path -> alert
   (3) indirection layer: /go/{slug} static redirect -> Google (one-line dead-link fix + server-side measure)
   + account governance: ONE governed Workspace account, 2FA, sharing-policy lock (else catalog-wide SPOF)
```

```
ENG DUAL VOICES — CONSENSUS TABLE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Dimension                       Claude    Codex    Consensus
  ──────────────────────────────── ───────── ───────── ─────────
  1. Architecture sound?           PARTIAL   PARTIAL  CONFIRMED (static core ok; 3 hidden subsystems unaccounted)
  2. Test coverage sufficient?     NO        NO       CONFIRMED (plan has ~none; 2 load-bearing tests absent)
  3. Performance risks addressed?  PARTIAL   PARTIAL  CONFIRMED (budgets stated; image reality + CLS unaddressed)
  4. Failure paths handled?        NO        NO       CONFIRMED (dead /copy unmonitored, no fallback, metric-blind)
  5. Deployment risk manageable?   YES       YES      CONFIRMED (static deploy genuinely low-risk)
  6. Data integrity?               NO        NO       CONFIRMED (model too thin; fake scores; two-source drift)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  6/6 CONFIRMED. Verdict (both): NOT ready to implement as written — sound foundation, but
  the parts that make it trustworthy/durable (external-dep monitoring, screenshot pipeline,
  unique content, the test harness) are left as hand-waves.
```

## Sections 1-9 (eng) — auto-decided fixes (all structural; scope never reduced, P2 boil-lakes)

- **§1 Architecture:** define a route manifest generated from typed data (`getStaticPaths`/`generateStaticParams` + `output:"export"`); build FAILS on duplicate slug, missing page, orphaned template, missing canonical/image/copy-link. Add the 3 hidden subsystems + `/go/{slug}` indirection + account governance. **(E1, E2, E8)**
- **§2 Error & Rescue Map (core runtime failure surface = Google Docs):**
  ```
  CODEPATH                  | FAILURE MODE                  | RESCUED? | RESCUE ACTION                       | USER SEES
  /go/{slug} -> Docs /copy  | Doc deleted/trashed           | N → fix  | health monitor flips status; CTA    | "being updated, try Word" (not 404)
  /go/{slug} -> Docs /copy  | permission drift (restricted) | N → fix  | disabled, .docx fallback shown      | DOCX download + view link
  /copy                     | login/consent wall            | N → fix  | document constraint; offer .docx    | no-login fallback
  build                     | REPLACE_WITH_* placeholder    | N → fix  | schema gate fails the build         | (never ships)
  Doc                       | edited after screenshot       | N → fix  | revision mismatch fails CI          | (page never lies)
  ```
- **§3 Security/threat:** low surface (static). Real items: governed Drive account (2FA, sharing-policy lock); email-capture spam/validation when added; trademark-in-URL is an accepted, owned risk.
- **§4 Data/edge:** zero-results, broken-link, mobile-preview edge cases covered in Design (Phase 2).
- **§5 Code quality:** demo.html clean; main smell is name-derived copy URLs (`placeholderCopyLink`) — never derive external Doc URLs from display names; store explicit `copy_url`.
- **§6 Tests:** full test diagram + artifact written to `~/.gstack/projects/google-docs-resume-template/wzb-main-test-plan-20260627.md`. The 2am test = scheduled copy-link health monitor (L5); runner-up = build-time placeholder gate (L0).
- **§7 Performance:** generate responsive variants (320/480/720/960 WebP/AVIF), `srcset`/`sizes`, explicit width/height (CLS), `loading="lazy"` below first row, budget initial image BYTES not just dims.
- **§8 Observability:** standardized event contract with `sendBeacon`/keepalive (don't block navigation); GSC API polling for "crawled-not-indexed"; health reports persisted as committed/artifact JSON. Monitors run via GitHub Actions / CF cron (static-compatible ops).
- **§9 Deploy:** static deploy low-risk; add 404, trailing-slash policy, generated sitemap auto-submit, canonical host check, build-determinism check.

## Required Outputs (Eng)

### NOT in scope
- 30 role pages at launch (CEO decision — thin/doorway risk); build the source-asset factory + 3 templates end-to-end first, then scale.
- Online editor / AI generator / payment / login (PRD already defers — correct).

### What already exists
- demo.html (UI-only reference; NOT an implementation seed — its client-render model must not be copied).
- Astro/Next static stack, Drive export endpoints, `sharp`, GitHub Actions / CF cron (all standard, reused not rebuilt).

### Failure Modes Registry (eng)
```
CODEPATH                 | FAILURE MODE              | RESCUED? | TEST? | USER SEES        | LOGGED?
-------------------------|---------------------------|----------|-------|------------------|--------
/go/{slug} -> /copy      | dead/revoked Doc          | after fix| L5    | degraded CTA     | health monitor + alert
build                    | REPLACE_WITH_* placeholder| after fix| L0    | (never ships)    | CI fail
screenshot               | stale after Doc edit      | after fix| L6    | (page can't lie) | CI/cron
analytics                | copy click lost on nav    | after fix| L9    | nothing          | sendBeacon assert
SEO                      | doorway/near-dup pages    | after fix| L3    | deindexed        | unique-title test + GSC
```
Pre-fix these were 5 CRITICAL GAPS (dead-link metric-blindness is the worst). All become covered once E1-E6 land.

## ENG COMPLETION SUMMARY
```
+====================================================================+
|            ENG REVIEW — COMPLETION SUMMARY                          |
+====================================================================+
| Dual voices          | Codex + Claude subagent — 6/6 CONFIRMED     |
| Verdict              | NOT ready as written (sound foundation)     |
| Architecture         | static core ok; 3 hidden subsystems added   |
| Test plan            | written to disk (9-row launch gate + diagram)|
| Failure modes        | 5 CRITICAL GAPS (all covered by E1-E6)       |
| Killer insight       | CTR metric is blind to dead-link failure    |
| Reversibility        | 5/5 (static, easy to pivot)                 |
| Eng taste decisions  | 0 (all findings structural)                 |
+====================================================================+
```
**Verdict:** Sound foundation + low-risk deploy, but the parts that make the product trustworthy
(external-dep health monitoring, screenshot pipeline, unique content, the test harness that
protects all three) are hand-waved. Resolve E1-E6 (esp. the link-health monitor + indirection
layer + build placeholder gate) and it becomes a low-risk shippable static project.

---

# CROSS-PHASE THEMES (flagged independently in 2+ phases — highest-confidence signals)

| Theme | Phases | Why it matters |
|-------|--------|----------------|
| **Fake "ATS 95" score** | CEO §3 + Design (trust) + Eng (data model) — **3 phases** | Invented authority in a high-anxiety category; trust/credibility landmine. Replace with transparent checklist + real parse evidence. |
| **Dead `/copy` link at conversion** | CEO (failure modes) + Design D2 + Eng E1 — **3 phases** | The product's #1 failure, unmonitored, no fallback, and invisible to the CTR metric. Needs health monitor + indirection + fallback. |
| **Client-rendered grid breaks the SEO premise** | Design D4 + Eng E4 — **2 phases** | Core SEO content invisible without JS; kills internal-link layer. Must be static/SSG with `<noscript>`. |
| **Template data model too thin** | Design D10 + Eng E7 — **2 phases** | Can't carry verification state, image dims, per-page SEO, or unique content. Define the real schema now. |
| **Thin/doorway content (30 role pages)** | CEO T3 + Eng E5 — **2 phases** | Near-duplicate pages = Helpful-Content/doorway risk on a new domain. Cut to ~5 verified, depth-before-breadth. |
| **Unrealistic / metric-blind success criteria** | CEO T6 + Eng E9 — **2 phases** | 90-day head-term targets are fantasy; CTR measures clicks not successful copies. Reset targets + measure copy success. |

# IMPLEMENTATION TASKS (aggregated across phases) — 29 total (19 P1, 10 P2)

## P1 — ship-blocking
- **T1** (growth/links) — Real link-acquisition plan (the binding ranking constraint)
- **T2** (content/ats) — Replace fake ATS score with transparent checklist + real PDF/DOCX screenshots
- **T3** (seo/scope) — Cut 30 role pages → ~5 verified (≥500 traffic potential)
- **T6** (seo/metrics) — Reset 30/90-day targets to indexation + a few KD2-3 long-tails in top 20
- **D1** (ui/card) — Real per-template screenshots replace skeletons (card + modal)
- **D2** (ui/copy) — Verified `/copy` links + per-link health state; broken link degrades gracefully
- **D3** (ui/states) — Empty/zero-results + loading states; concise aria-live count
- **D4** (seo/render) — Static/SSG grid + `<noscript>`; hydrate filtering on top
- **D5** (a11y/modal) — Modal focus trap/restore/inert/Escape
- **D10** (data/schema) — Define real template data schema
- **E1** (infra/link-health) — Scheduled copy-link health monitor off the deploy path + alerts (THE 2am test)
- **E2** (infra/redirect) — `/go/{slug}` redirect indirection layer
- **E3** (build/data) — Build-blocking schema gate (reject REPLACE_WITH_* / dup slug / missing asset)
- **E4** (seo/render) — Lock SSG contract; no-JS crawl test; demo is UI-only, not a seed
- **E5** (seo/canonical) — Canonical graph + page-type rules; resolve `/` vs `/google-docs-resume-template/`
- **E6** (pipeline/screenshots) — Reproducible Doc→WebP pipeline + revision/freshness check
- **E7** (data/schema) — Extend schema (doc_id, revision, status, img dims/alt/srcset, faq[], bullet/guidance, related[])
- **E9** (analytics) — `copy_google_docs_click` via sendBeacon before nav + Playwright assertion
- **E12** (process) — Build 3 templates end-to-end before scaling to 24; depth-before-breadth indexing

## P2 — same-branch / fast-follow
- **T4** (growth) — Promote distribution-first seeding (Reddit/Pinterest/YouTube/public Doc) to P0
- **T5** (product) — Evaluate one interactive tool (ATS checker / JD→bullet) as differentiator + link magnet
- **D6** (a11y) — Remove `outline:none`; `:focus-visible` ring; prefers-reduced-motion guard
- **D7** (a11y) — Filter chips `aria-pressed`; dedicated hidden live region
- **D8** (ui) — Mobile nav replacement (hamburger/menu)
- **D9** (content) — Delete internal strategy/TODO text from user-facing copy
- **D11** (seo) — Generate JSON-LD ItemList from the same source as rendered cards
- **E8** (infra) — Governed single Google Workspace owner account (2FA, sharing-policy lock)
- **E10** (perf) — Responsive image variants + srcset/sizes + width/height (CLS) + byte budget
- **E11** (build) — Generate sitemap/robots/canonical/OG/JSON-LD from route manifest; CI sitemap diff

# FINAL STATUS
- **CEO:** SELECTIVE EXPANSION; premise gate → **B (hybrid)** chosen by user; 6/6 dual-voice consensus.
- **Design:** 3/10 prototype; 7/7 consensus; 12 structural fixes auto-included; 2 taste decisions open.
- **Eng:** NOT-ready-as-written → becomes low-risk once E1-E6 land; 6/6 consensus; test plan on disk.
- **DX:** skipped (consumer product).
- **Open taste decisions (final gate):** DT1 directory-first layout, DT2 distinctive visual identity.
- **Backlog:** 29 tasks (19 P1, 10 P2). Reversibility 5/5.
